name: Bridge Extended Proofs: SSA + SyGuS Contraction + Classical-Correlation

pytorch.reason: not needed -- no autograd layer in this proof sim

pyg.reason: not needed -- no graph layer in this proof sim

z3.reason: Core proof engine for Proof A (SSA UNSAT) and Proof C (classical-correlation UNSAT). Each impossibility claim is encoded as a z3 Real-arithmetic formula; UNSAT confirms no counterexample survived the full constraint system.

cvc5.reason: SyGuS synthesis engine for Proof B: synthesizes the boundary function f(p, lambda) = lambda + p - 1 for the L4 depolarizing contraction manifold.

sympy.reason: Symbolic cross-checks: SSA derivation from concavity, and classical-correlation bound MI <= S_A verified symbolically.

clifford.reason: not needed -- no geometric algebra layer here

geomstats.reason: not needed -- no manifold geometry here

e3nn.reason: not needed -- no equivariant network layer here

rustworkx.reason: not needed -- no dependency graph here

xgi.reason: not needed -- no hypergraph layer here

toponetx.reason: not needed -- no cell complex here

gudhi.reason: not needed -- no persistent homology here

proof_A_strong_subadditivity.claim: Strong Subadditivity violation S(ABC) + S(B) > S(AB) + S(BC) is impossible under the full entropy axiom system including SSA.
proof_A_strong_subadditivity.encoding_note: SSA is encoded as axiom alongside non-negativity, subadditivity, Araki-Lieb, monotonicity, and upper bounds. The negated SSA is then asserted. Sanity SAT check drops SSA to confirm non-vacuity.

sympy_cross_check.conclusion: SSA violation S(ABC)+S(B) > S(AB)+S(BC) is equivalent to I(A:C|B) < 0 -- impossible for quantum states (concavity of vN entropy).

proof_A_strong_subadditivity.interpretation: UNSAT main: no entropy assignment satisfying all quantum axioms (including SSA) can produce a SSA violation. SAT sanity: without SSA the violation is achievable, confirming the proof is non-trivial. Proof A survived.

proof_B_sygus_contraction_boundary.claim: cvc5 SyGuS can synthesize the boundary function f(p, lambda) for the L4 depolarizing CPTP contraction manifold satisfying: f(p, 1-p) = 0 (boundary) and f(0, 0) < 0 (interior).
proof_B_sygus_contraction_boundary.solution_interpretation: Synthesized f(p, lam) = p + lam - 1, equivalently lam - (1-p). This is exactly the signed distance to the boundary lambda = 1-p: f = 0 on boundary, f < 0 in the admissible interior (lam < 1-p), f > 0 in the forbidden region (lam > 1-p).
proof_B_sygus_contraction_boundary.interpretation: SyGuS found: (lambda ((p Real) (lam Real)) (- (+ p lam) 1.0)). This is the boundary polynomial lambda + p - 1 = 0 for the L4 contraction manifold. Proof B survived.

proof_C_classical_correlation_no_pos_Ic.claim: A classically-correlated (zero-discord) state with MI > 0 cannot have I_c = S_B - S_AB > 0. Classical correlation bound MI <= S_A forces I_c <= 0.
proof_C_classical_correlation_no_pos_Ic.encoding_note: S_AB = S_A + S_B - MI (definition). MI > 0 (classical correlations present). MI <= S_A (zero-discord bound: classical correlation bounded by marginal). Negated claim: I_c = MI - S_A > 0. Sanity SAT: entangled states can have I_c > 0.

sympy_cross_check.conclusion: I_c = MI - S_A <= 0 for all valid zero-discord states

proof_C_classical_correlation_no_pos_Ic.interpretation: UNSAT main: no zero-discord state with MI > 0 can achieve I_c > 0 -- the classical-correlation bound forecloses it. SAT sanity: entangled states with S_AB < S_B are achievable, confirming the UNSAT is caused specifically by the zero-discord constraint. Proof C survived.

ghz_state_satisfies_ssa.note: GHZ entropy assignment (S_ABC=0, S_B=S_AB=S_BC=1) satisfies SSA.

classical_state_Ic_leq_0_is_sat.note: Classical state MI=0.3, S_A=0.8, S_B=0.7 has I_c = MI - S_A = -0.5 <= 0.

ssa_satisfied_is_sat.note: SSA S(ABC)+S(B) <= S(AB)+S(BC) is satisfiable -- the allowed regime.

entangled_state_Ic_positive_is_sat.note: Entangled state with S_AB < S_B (I_c > 0) is satisfiable -- allowed for entangled.

depolarizing_forbidden_region_f_positive.note: f(0.3, 0.9) = 0.2000 > 0: this (p, lambda) pair is in the forbidden region lambda > 1-p.

tight_ssa_markov_chain_is_sat.note: Tight SSA (Markov chain) equality is satisfiable -- achieved by Markov states.

classical_tight_bound_Ic_zero_is_sat.note: MI = S_A (tight zero-discord) gives I_c = 0 exactly -- boundary case is SAT.

sygus_result.interpretation: Synthesized f(p, lam) = p + lam - 1, equivalently lam - (1-p). This is exactly the signed distance to the boundary lambda = 1-p: f = 0 on boundary, f < 0 in the admissible interior (lam < 1-p), f > 0 in the forbidden region (lam > 1-p).
